methods.description: This method describes the collection, chemical processing, and microscopic analysis of surface sediment samples from outcrops to extract, identify, and quantify palynomorphs, particularly dinocysts, for palaeoenvironmental and biostratigraphic interpretation.

steps.description: Surface sediment samples were collected at 28 outcrops during the research project entitled "Basic Geological Map of Republic Croatia 1: 50000". Sampling density was adjusted to the thickness of the layers with the same lithological and palaeontological characteristics. Each change was sampled, and 98 samples were prepared.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface sediment samples from geological outcrops based on lithological and palaeontological characteristics

steps.description: The samples were treated with cold HCl (15%), removing carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove carbonates from sediment samples using cold 15% HCl

steps.description: The samples were treated with... HF (40%), removing... silica.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove silica from sediment samples using 40% HF

steps.description: Heavy liquid (ZnCl 2 , s.g. 2,1kg/l) was used to separate the organic matter from the undissolved inorganic components.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate organic matter from inorganic residue using heavy liquid separation with ZnCl2 (s.g. 2.1 kg/l)

steps.description: The residue obtained was sieved through a 20-µm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve palynological residue using a 20-µm sieve

steps.description: palynological slides were prepared using glycerin gelatin as the mounting medium.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare palynological slides using glycerin gelatin

steps.description: The samples were not oxidized at any stage.
Analysis of palynological slides was performed under the light microscope combined with the interference contrast, resulting in identifi cation of 88 palynomorph taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify palynomorph taxa using light microscopy with interference contrast
outputs: List of identified palynomorph taxa (88 taxa)

steps.description: A fl uorescence technique was used to distinguish in situ forms from reworked palynomorphs.
steps.searchable_summary: Differentiate in situ and reworked palynomorphs using fluorescence microscopy
outputs: Classification of palynomorphs as in situ or reworked

steps.description: Each slide was counted for 200 palynomorphs for the purpose of quantitative analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quantitative counting of palynomorphs (200 per slide)
outputs: Quantitative counts of palynomorphs per slide
